items.title: Launcher overlay toggle-launcher message handler lacks sender.id origin check
items.action: Add a sender parameter to the overlay onMessage listener at line 984 and guard with `if (sender.id !== chrome.runtime.id) return;` mirroring background/index.ts and bus-adapter.ts.
items.rationale: Confirmed: the content-script listener accepts no sender, so any web page can sendMessage a synthetic toggle-launcher and drive overlay toggle()/suggestion fetch with attacker-supplied windowId — a real exploitable origin-trust gap that every sibling listener already guards.

items.title: Saved-tab navigation sinks skip the http(s) scheme guard that every sibling open path enforces
items.action: Add an http(s) new URL() scheme check before chrome.tabs.update/create in openSavedTab (both branches) and goHome, and refine originalURL/currentURL in schemas.ts to require http(s) at the storage/backup boundary.
items.rationale: Confirmed unguarded at pinned-tabs.ts:39/49; a crafted imported backup can seed javascript:/data: into originalURL that reaches chrome.tabs.update/create on open or go-home, violating the codebase's own authoritative-scheme-guard invariant.

items.title: Launcher overlay parses SW suggestion response with bare type casts — no Zod validation
items.action: Add a manual per-item guard (e.g. require typeof r.id/r.url === 'string') or a zero-import filter in launcher/shared before assigning payload.results/openUrls/ungrantedSources, mirroring messages.ts safeParse.
items.rationale: Confirmed Array.isArray-only casts at lines 717-721; this is the only copy of the suggestion-parse path and omits the Zod validation the shared helper enforces, so a malformed SW response renders or dispatches wrong-typed objects at a runtime trust boundary.

items.title: Options surfaces read raw chrome.storage.local and rely on partial structural casts instead of using readPersistedState()
items.action: Replace direct chrome.storage.local.get(STATE_STORAGE_KEY) reads in these options surfaces with readPersistedState() and use its kind result; use ArchivedTabSchema.array().safeParse in RecentlyArchived.
items.rationale: Recurring trust-boundary gap (prev rank 3): bypassing the envelope + migration + salvage layer means a pre-v7 on-disk format fails AppStateV7Schema.safeParse silently and degrades all three surfaces to empty state with no migration.

items.title: Internal JS modules exposed as web-accessible resources to all origins
items.action: Inline the overlay/boundary dynamic imports so crxjs need not split internal chunks as WAR, or set use_dynamic_url:true for those asset entries to randomize URLs and reduce fingerprinting.
items.rationale: Stable WAR chunk URLs let any page fingerprint Lunma and read its internal boundary/search-engine logic; information-disclosure only, not code-exec, so it ranks below the data-validation and scheme issues.

items.title: store.state as SidebarLocalState pattern: AppState and SidebarLocalState are incompatible casts
items.action: Declare the sidebar-side store state as the existing SidebarState (AppState & SidebarLocalState) intersection so the as SidebarLocalState casts disappear, or change the casts to AppState & SidebarLocalState.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 9): the optional-field narrowing is a type-level lie spread across three files where renamed/retyped local fields fail silently on core sidebar state.

items.title: SmartFolder.svelte $effect mutates $state object properties directly, bypassing Svelte 5 reactivity
items.action: Replace in-place lastSeenById[item.id]=item / delete lastSeenById[id] with whole-object reassignment ({ ...lastSeenById, [id]: item } and a spread-then-delete copy) so deletions are tracked by the $state proxy.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 37): if the Svelte version doesn't track deleteProperty on $state proxies, removed feed items stay rendered as displayItems reads stale entries — a correctness defect on a user-facing surface.

items.title: DEFAULTS object constructed with `as unknown as Settings` — Zod cannot validate it
items.action: Replace the Object.fromEntries + `satisfies Partial<Settings> as unknown as Settings` with an explicitly typed object literal `export const DEFAULTS: Settings = {...}`.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 36): the unknown-bridge lets a SettingDeclaration whose default mismatches its declared type pass compilation, surfacing only as a runtime DEFAULTS type error.

items.title: FeedSubscriptions.svelte and Options.svelte use onMount(async …) without cancellation guard
items.action: Wrap both async onMount bodies in the cancelled-flag pattern from App.svelte:74-92, returning a cleanup that sets cancelled=true.
items.rationale: Late writes to stale $state after unmount are a latent leak and a source of confusing test behaviour, but Svelte tolerates them so impact is low.

items.title: space.color as SpaceColor cast is unsafe — color field is already typed SpaceColor in AppState
items.action: Remove all four `as SpaceColor` casts in App.svelte and fix any resulting widening at the SpaceSchema.color inference level instead.
items.rationale: The casts are redundant today but suppress future schema-inference regressions; low risk because the field is already correctly typed.

items.title: biome-ignore suppressions for noExplicitAny in production-adjacent test helpers hide typing gaps
items.action: Replace the `store as any` spy casts with a narrow mutator-name type alias so renaming a mutator fails compilation.
items.rationale: Suppression follows policy but the name-string spy targets drift silently on rename; test-only scope keeps it low priority.

items.title: biome.json launcher/shared override missing guard against launcher surface imports
items.action: Add **/launcher/newtab, **/launcher/newtab/**, **/launcher/overlay, and **/launcher/overlay* to the launcher/shared override's noRestrictedImports patterns in biome.json.
items.rationale: No current violation but the shared-grade no-surface contract at docs/architecture.md:727 is unenforced, so a future newtab/overlay import into the search engine would pass lint.

items.title: overlay.ts allowed to import any ui/ file by biome; only the budget test enforces the ui/favicon-only constraint
items.action: Add a biome override for launcher/overlay.ts banning **/ui/** except !**/ui/favicon, or assert the allowed ui/ module list in the overlay budget test.
items.rationale: A pure-TS ui/ helper imported by overlay.ts would pass the bundle budget yet violate the single sanctioned launcher→ui edge with no lint error; low risk as no violation exists.

items.title: biome.json second background override excludes coordinator and group-orchestrator but not boundary-controller
items.action: Add !apps/extension/src/background/boundary-controller.ts to the exclusion list in the second background override in biome.json.
items.rationale: Tooling lists only two of the three documented orchestration collaborators; no current violation but boundary-controller would be wrongly blocked if it ever needed a handler import.

items.title: Hard-coded design values in sidebar/main.ts error-state HTML bypass the token contract
items.action: Replace font-size:13px, padding:6px 16px, border-radius:6px in the inline boot-failure HTML with token vars (var(--text-sm), var(--radius-sm), etc.).
items.rationale: Raw pixel values bypass the token contract in a .ts file Stylelint cannot reach; only the rarely-seen boot-failure fallback is affected.

items.title: handlers/chrome-tabs.ts — tabs.onUpdated boundary re-arm and smart-item re-arm paths have no unit tests
items.action: In coordinator.handlers.test.ts add tests asserting configureBoundary fires on status:complete for a bound saved tab, configureSmartItemBoundary fires for a matching smart binding slot, and neither fires on status:loading.
items.rationale: Two side-effect re-arm paths fixing the documented stale-boundary-after-reload bug have zero coverage, so a regression would be invisible in CI.

items.title: e2e — launcher overlay has no test for search input, result selection, or Enter-to-navigate
items.action: Add an e2e test that opens the overlay via Alt+L, dispatches a synthetic input into the shadow-root search field (or a data-testid reflecting result count), and verifies Enter closes the overlay.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 17): the launcher's primary flow — type, get results, navigate — has no real-Chrome coverage, so closed-shadow input wiring regressions go undetected.

items.title: background/connectors/connector.ts — boundedFetch timeout and abort behavior not tested
items.action: Create connector.test.ts testing boundedFetch: immediate resolve returns response, a hanging fetch aborts after FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS (fake timers), and the abort signal rejects with AbortError.
items.rationale: The 20s timeout/abort guarding all four connectors is never exercised, so a refactor could silently disable timeout enforcement.

items.title: handlers/spaces.ts — renameSpace and recolourSpace rollback not tested via the handler module
items.action: Create spaces.test.ts with a HandlerContext stub whose groups.propagateGroupIdentity rejects, asserting the store name reverts before the throw and the error mentions renameSpace; mirror for recolourSpace.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 12): the atomic rollback contract is only covered through the full coordinator fixture, leaving the handler-level revert path without isolated assertion.

items.title: handlers/chrome-groups-windows.ts — tabGroups.onUpdated disambiguation re-title failure path untested
items.action: In coordinator.tab-groups.test.ts add a test where the disambiguating chrome.tabGroups.update throws, asserting the drain still acks ok, the disambiguated name persists, and markDirty is called.
items.rationale: The swallow-and-debug catch on re-title failure is dead code in the suite, so a broken disambiguation-recovery path would not surface.

items.title: background/handlers/backup.ts — importState post-replace reconciliation failure path untested
items.action: In coordinator.backup.test.ts add a test where chrome.tabs.query rejects after a valid import, asserting state was replaced, markDirty fired, and the coordinator does not crash.
items.rationale: markDirty runs before the try/catch so a partial reconciliation still persists; the failure path that proves state survives is untested.

items.title: shared/schemas.ts — AppStateV7Schema PinNodeSchema 'smart' variant not exercised in schemas.test.ts
items.action: Add a 'PinNodeSchema — smart variant' describe in schemas.test.ts: valid smart node parses, missing source rejects, unknown source string rejects.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 13): the smart discriminated-union branch on the persistence trust boundary has no direct schema parse test.

items.title: handlers/temp-tabs.ts — clearTempTabs pruneArchivedTabs cap not exercised; newTab cross-Space path untested
items.action: In coordinator.tab-groups.test.ts seed 99 archivedTabs + clear 5 asserting length==100 (cap), and add a newTab test where payload.spaceId differs asserting orchestrateActivation runs before tabs.create.
items.rationale: The archive-cap pruning and the cross-Space new-tab activation branch are both unexercised correctness paths on frequent user actions.

items.title: handlers/activation.ts — activateSpaceInWindow idempotency not directly tested
items.action: Add activation.test.ts asserting an already-active spaceId makes zero store mutations and zero orchestrateActivation calls, and a different spaceId invokes both exactly once.
items.rationale: A regression in the early-return guard would double-orchestrate on every same-Space activation, currently only checked incidentally via comments.

items.title: shared/store.svelte.ts — setActiveTab RSS consumed-tab return value not tested at store level
items.action: In store.live-tabs.test.ts add tests: no smartItemBindings returns [], a consuming activation returns the slot tabId, and an already-read item does not re-return it.
items.rationale: The store method's consumed-tab contract is only covered E2E via the coordinator, leaving the return-value boundary untested at the unit level.

items.title: e2e — no test for space deletion, rename, or trash/restore flows
items.action: Add space-lifecycle.spec.ts covering deleteSpace removing the chip, restoreSpaceFromTrash re-adding it, and renameSpace updating the chip name, using existing dispatch/readState helpers.
items.rationale: Destructive broadcast→reconcile round-trips for these mutations cannot be verified by unit tests alone and have no e2e coverage.

items.title: handlers/temp-tabs.ts — openUrl blocked-scheme path tested but unparseable-URL warning path coverage is narrow
items.action: Add openUrl tests asserting chrome-extension:// and data: URLs are blocked (no tabs.create, acks ok) in coordinator.handlers.test.ts.
items.rationale: Parseable-but-non-http(s) schemes aren't asserted, so a future widening of the allowlist would slip through; existing javascript:/ftp: coverage limits the gap.

items.title: handlers/queries.ts — spaceExists function has no unit test
items.action: Add a spaceExists describe in queries.test.ts: true for a present id, false for unknown, false for empty spaces.
items.rationale: A pure predicate guarding seven handlers is only tested indirectly; trivial isolated coverage closes the gap.

items.title: handlers/smart-folders.ts — markSmartItemRead handler has no coordinator-level test
items.action: Add a markSmartItemRead describe in coordinator.smart-folders.test.ts: valid marks read + acks ok, already-read is idempotent, unknown folderId is a no-op.
items.rationale: The explicit per-item read command lacks coordinator coverage while sibling smart-folder commands have it; low risk as the store no-ops gracefully.

items.title: identity.yml shipped with no owning task in any active change
items.action: Add a retrospective checked task to open-source-public-launch tasks §1 documenting identity.yml landed in commit e0d9cdc, its job name identity, and its §4.1 required-check role.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 22): a file named and relied on by an active change shipped without a checkbox — an undocumented deviation violating the doc-lockstep policy.

items.title: Stale file-level comment in release-please.yml contradicts implemented CWS step
items.action: Update the line-11 comment to state the CWS publish step (lines 81-93, gated on HAS_CWS) is implemented, referencing extension-store-publish.
items.rationale: Doc-lockstep drift: the comment claims the CWS upload is deferred while the step ships in the same file, misleading future readers.

items.title: docs/tech-stack.md pins stale release-please-action@v4 while workflow uses @v5
items.action: Update docs/tech-stack.md lines 131 and 150 to reference release-please-action@v5.
items.rationale: A Dependabot bump ran outside any change, leaving the pinned-version doc out of lockstep with the shipped workflow.

items.title: github-repo-and-ci task 7.2 wording says 'shared' dragTo but only pin-temp-into-folder.spec.ts was updated
items.action: Either clarify task 7.2 wording to say it targeted only pin-temp-into-folder.spec.ts, or extend the assert-and-retry dragTo to pinned-tabs.spec.ts and boundary.spec.ts.
items.rationale: The 'shared' wording is misleading and the other two single-attempt dragTo helpers retain potential CI flakiness; option (b) is safer but low impact.

items.title: github-repo-and-ci tasks §5.4 and §6.4 remain open but are correctly deferred
items.action: No immediate action; confirm the dependency note on open-source-public-launch §3.2/§4.1 is present in both changes.
items.rationale: Correctly plan-blocked deferrals (paid-plan branch protection); included only to confirm the change cannot archive until the public flip.

items.title: open-source-public-launch §3 and §4–§5 are unchecked and gated — correctly pending
items.action: No action; archive extension-store-publish once §1–§2 verify green, leaving maintainer-owned §3 open.
items.rationale: Correctly gated pending the first CWS publish and semver-enforcement; documented as no-op for completeness.

items.title: newtab.css: --stagger-4 at 440ms produces 640ms total entrance motion for .below section
items.action: Reduce --stagger-4 (and proportionally the stagger scale) so the full entrance delay+duration stays within ~400ms in newtab.css.
items.rationale: 640ms total motion far exceeds the 150-250ms motion bar in CLAUDE.md for the non-reduced baseline experience — the highest-impact visual finding.

items.title: overlay.css: bare border-radius literals instead of declared token vars
items.action: Replace the literal border-radius:6px with var(--r-sm) at lines 163/239/414/451 and 8px with var(--r-md) at line 475 in overlay.css.
items.rationale: Both radius tokens are already declared in :host, so these literals will silently drift from the token scale on any radius update.

items.title: overlay.css: --favicon-size not in :host — favicon and space-dot hard-coded at 16px/7px
items.action: Add --favicon-size:16px to the :host token block and use it in .favicon; document or tokenize the 7px space-dot in overlay.css.
items.rationale: Missing shadow-root token mirror means the favicon size diverges silently from the canonical token despite the sync comment.

items.title: SpaceEditor.svelte: orphaned light-theme accent overrides with no corresponding token scope
items.action: Remove the dead :root[data-theme='light'] .panel oklch block, or gate it on an OpenSpec change introducing a light-theme token scope.
items.rationale: Recurring (prev rank 47): hand-rolled oklch literals for an unwired light theme would be consulted before any future tokens, bypassing the system.

items.title: SpaceSwitcher.svelte: raw 'ease' timing function instead of --ease-standard token
items.action: Change the background-color transition on line 293 from the keyword ease to var(--ease-standard).
items.rationale: Deviates from the design system's standard cubic-bezier curve that every other transition uses; trivial single-line fix.

items.title: ColorSwatch.svelte: hard-coded oklch(0 0 0 / 0.18) inset ring — no shadow/border token
items.action: Replace the raw inset oklch ring with var(--border) or introduce a --border-overlay token in tokens.css and reference it.
items.rationale: A raw colour literal on a ui/ primitive violates the token contract for shared primitives; low visual stakes.

items.title: overlay.css: z-index: 2147483647 magic number — --z-launcher not mirrored in :host
items.action: Add --z-launcher:2147483647 to the :host token block and replace the raw z-index on .scrim line 98 with var(--z-launcher).
items.rationale: A raw max-int magic number bypasses the z-index token scale; isolated and low risk.

items.title: newtab.css and Options.svelte: glass-bg neutral override duplicated in three places with no shared token
items.action: Introduce --glass-bg-subtle and --glass-bg-strong-subtle tokens in tokens.css and reference them in all three locations.
items.rationale: Three identical copy-pasted glass fills risk silent drift, but the duplication is documented and low-impact.

items.title: DragClone.svelte: direct --glass-bg-strong usage bypasses Surface primitive
items.action: Wrap .fav-clone in Surface variant='glass', or use var(--surface-2) and comment why only the opaque fill applies.
items.rationale: Partial glass (fill without blur/border/highlight) produces an inconsistent frosted surface; isolated to the drag clone.

items.title: sidebar/app.css: bare z-index: 1 without a token
items.action: Introduce a --z-local:1 token for intra-component stacking or add a comment documenting the off-scale value on line 274.
items.rationale: A bespoke stacking value outside the token scale, but it only creates a local context with no cross-surface effect.

items.title: SmartFolderNode type declared in three places
items.action: Canonicalise `export type SmartFolderNode = Extract<PinNode, { kind: 'smart' }>` in shared/types.ts and replace the three exports and five local aliases with imports.
items.rationale: One concept defined eight ways; any PinNode 'smart' shape change must be re-applied across files — the highest-impact duplication finding.

items.title: SpaceColor palette array copy-pasted four times
items.action: Import the existing SPACE_COLORS from shared/schemas.ts in bus.ts and the three sidebar files, dropping the private copies.
items.rationale: A nine-element palette duplicated five ways means any colour addition or reorder must be replicated everywhere — a real drift hazard.

items.title: Inline triple-nested loop for smart-item boundary re-arm in tabs.onUpdated handler
items.action: Add a smartItemBindingForTab(tabId) query on LunmaStore (or queries.ts) doing the triple scan once, and call it from both sites.
items.rationale: The same O(n^3) byItem→slots walk is duplicated; binding counts are small so this is de-duplication more than a perf concern.

items.title: 30-day retention expressed as an inline arithmetic literal in two separate files
items.action: Use the existing ARCHIVE_TTL_MS in trash-purge.ts and extract a shared DAY_MS constant imported by auto-archive.ts and RecentlyArchived.svelte.
items.rationale: Duplicated time arithmetic where a constant already exists; low risk but easy correctness-of-meaning win.

items.title: SETTINGS_KEY string 'lunma.settings' re-declared in overlay.ts to avoid import
items.action: Move SETTINGS_KEY into a Zod-free shared/storage-keys.ts and import it in both settings.ts and overlay.ts.
items.rationale: A storage-key literal in two places risks divergence on rename; the no-Zod constraint is satisfied by a plain-string module.

items.title: DEBOUNCE_MS = 120 defined independently in overlay.ts and NewTab.svelte
items.action: Extract export const LAUNCHER_DEBOUNCE_MS = 120 into launcher/shared and import it in both files, verifying no bundle bloat for overlay.ts.
items.rationale: Same debounce value duplicated within one surface; both must stay in sync but impact is minor.

items.title: REFRESH_MINUTES_DEFAULT exported but never imported
items.action: Delete the unused REFRESH_MINUTES_DEFAULT export, or move it (plus an RSS variant) to shared/ and consume it in SmartFolderEditor.svelte's defaultRefresh.
items.rationale: A dead export plus an inline re-derivation of the same default; cleanup only.

items.title: 1440 magic number for minutes-per-day in FirstRunNotice.svelte
items.action: Introduce const MINUTES_PER_DAY = 1440 at the top of FirstRunNotice.svelte and replace both literal occurrences.
items.rationale: A bare unit magic number with no readability cost beyond clarity; lowest-impact code-quality item.

items.title: Section-header banners in source files that duplicate module or block names
items.action: Remove WHAT-only banners and keep/convert to JSDoc only those carrying a non-obvious WHY or cross-reference.
items.rationale: Pure comment noise that adds no rationale; trivial cleanup with no functional effect.

items.title: optional_host_permissions grants all-hosts (https://*/* and http://*/*)
items.action: Replace standing https://*/* and http://*/* in manifest.json with gesture-bound per-origin grants derived from baseUrl via originPatternForBaseUrl, or document the threat model if a wildcard is unavoidable.
items.rationale: A single user-approved all-hosts grant exposes ambient GitLab/Jira cookies to every site, a broad SSRF/exfiltration surface and CWS-review liability that undercuts the codebase's least-privilege posture.

items.title: All 4 connector modules eagerly imported in hot SW boot path
items.action: Convert the top-level connector imports in smart-folders.ts:6-9 to a lazy Map<source, () => Promise<SourceConnector>> registry loaded on first fetchSmartFolderRuntime() of that source.
items.rationale: ~52KB/~11KB-gzip parsed on every SW boot for users who may use no connectors — the only high-severity startup cost and the clearest perf win.

items.title: Sidebar mounts all UI components eagerly without code splitting
items.action: Lazy-load conditional feature components (SmartFolderEditor, SpaceEditor) in App.svelte via Svelte dynamic import, keeping primitives eager, and measure bundle impact.
items.rationale: 18 static imports inflate initial sidebar parse/eval even for panels never opened — the most impactful surface for code splitting, but needs measurement.

items.title: Synchronous settings read during sidebar boot blocks initial render
items.action: Parallelize the await readSettings() in main.ts:46 with the state snapshot via Promise.all and apply tint/boundary post-mount, keeping only density pre-mount if layout-shift-critical.
items.rationale: Serial storage.sync read gates first paint on cold boot; parallelizing is a clear latency win on the most-used surface.

items.title: No SW resource batching for frequent Chrome API operations
items.action: Collapse the two boot-path tab queries into one chrome.tabs.query({}) and have writeSetting (settings.ts:368-379) merge an in-memory settings cache instead of re-reading all settings on each single-field write.
items.rationale: Redundant IPC/scheduler overhead on boot and every settings write; batching cuts repeated round-trips with modest effort.

items.title: overlay.css re-declares base tokens with hard-coded oklch values instead of inheriting
items.action: Add a parity test (like space-palette-parity.test.ts) failing on drift for tokens that must match tokens.css, and annotate intentionally-different values (scrim/glass opacity) in overlay.css.
items.rationale: Shadow-root token copies can silently drift from tokens.css with no guard; a parity test bounds the divergence on a colour-critical surface.

items.title: New-tab launcher makes synchronous readSettings before mount
items.action: Move the readSettings() await in newtab/main.ts:45 into a Promise.all with the state snapshot so reads run in parallel and mount can use defaults.
items.rationale: Same boot-blocking pattern as the sidebar but on a transient surface, so lower impact.

items.title: No lazy loading for options surface components
items.action: Defer non-critical options sections (Backup & restore, Feed subscriptions, Recently archived, Advanced) behind scroll/expand, keeping the primary Settings section eager.
items.rationale: Options opens infrequently so parse-cost bloat is the least-visible perf concern.

items.title: seedExistingTabs builds full spaceByGroupByWindow map on every boot without memoization
items.action: Optionally cache the spaceByGroupByWindow map in store.state to avoid rebuilding; lower priority than connector lazy-loading.
items.rationale: O(n*m) work happens once per boot with bounded impact, the weakest perf item.

items.title: Boundary-divert listener forwards page-controlled URL into openUrl (mitigated)
items.action: No change today; keep openUrl's http(s) re-validation authoritative and add an explicit scheme check at the index.ts:238 enqueue site if openUrl is ever refactored to trust callers.
items.rationale: Already defended by sender.id check and the openUrl sink guard; flagged only as a single bus-bypassing path to keep hardened.

items.title: Connector PATs stored unencrypted in chrome.storage.local (expected for platform)
items.action: Add a test asserting tokens never appear in broadcastState payloads or logs and document local-plaintext storage in the options UI.
items.rationale: Inherent MV3 constraint with a disciplined single-accessor module and no code defect; only hygiene-maintenance remains.
